pages.title: Brooms in the Boiler Room
pages.tagline: Dust yourself off before the brooms arrive.

pages.body: Step into a black-and-white tower where greed turns cleaning tools against their owners. This frantic track blends gritty 90s boom bap breaks with dramatic strings and ominous bassoons to create a Halloween noir atmosphere that feels raw yet dangerously playful. As water rises and stars fill the eyes of innocent mice, an old spell goes awry, flooding the room in a cartoonish horror show. The music marches forward like an unstoppable army, capturing the slippery inevitability of losing control over one's own creation.
pages.styleSentence: A frantic 100 BPM dark orchestral jazz rap track blending gritty 90s East Coast boom bap drum breaks with dramatic strings, ominous bassoons, prominent oboe lines, and marching broomstick percussion to create a Halloween noir cartoon atmosphere that feels both raw and dangerously playful.

pages.text:
[Intro - Old Film Projector, Wizard Tower]

[film reel clicking]
[thunder rolling]
[low bassoon riff]
[staccato strings creeping]

Voice 1 - Raspy:
Ayo...

Voice 1:
Don’t touch that hat.

Voice 2 - Crazy Whisper:
Heh... too late.

Both:
Brooms in the boiler room.

[Chorus - Raspy Gang Chant]

Brooms in the boiler room,
water starts to rise,
little mouse grinning with the stars in his eyes.

Brooms in the boiler room,
knocking at the door,
spell got loose and it hungry for more.

Black-and-white world,
but the money still gold,
whole tower shaking when the wild drums roll.

Brooms in the boiler room,
moon turned blue,
one wrong spell and the whole crew doomed.

[Verse 1 - Rapper 1 - Raspy, Unhinged]

I came through small with the hat pulled low,
stars on the brim and the gloves still glow.

Strings go stab,
bassoon go low,

broom got a bucket and it march like woah.

Told me don’t touch,
so I touched that quick,

now the whole tower sick and the floorboards shift.

One broom, two brooms,
three more rise,

black ink magic in the cartoon eyes.

Timpani boom when the stairwell crack,

oboe line howlin’ and the shadows clap back.

Moonlight bends when the wand goes snap,

shadow on the wall with a long-eared cap.

I ain’t scared,
I’m just deep in the trick,

too much power for a little dude slick.

Black-and-white frame,
but the gold still gleam,

money in the fog and the magic in steam.

[Pre-Chorus - Call and Response]

Voice 1:
Who cast that?

Voice 2:
Not me.

Voice 1:
Who touched the hat?

Voice 2:
Not me.

Voice 1:
Who flooded the room?

Voice 2:
Couldn’t be me.

Both:
Then why the broom got a whole army?

[Chorus - Raspy Gang Chant]

Brooms in the boiler room,
water starts to rise,
little mouse grinning with the stars in his eyes.

Brooms in the boiler room,
knocking at the door,
spell got loose and it hungry for more.

Black-and-white world,
but the money still gold,
whole tower shaking when the wild drums roll.

Brooms in the boiler room,
moon turned blue,
one wrong spell and the whole crew doomed.

[Verse 2 - Rapper 2 - Raspy, Crazed]

I seen the mop grow teeth in the midnight haze,

seen buckets pour rivers through a hundred stairways.

Woodwinds laughing,
trumpets go crooked,

whole magic tower looking haunted and flooded.

Old wizard gone,
but the hat stayed watching,

every little sparkle turned toxic.

Broomstick army with the wooden-leg stomp,

tap dance terror when the bassline thump.

I told little homie,
“You don’t know that spell.”

He said,
“I know enough to make the whole room swell.”

Then the strings start chasing,
ceiling got eyes,

stars in the robe started falling from the sky.

Cauldron bubble,
floorboards split,

one more chant and the whole place flipped.

Magic ain’t cute when the shadows get tall,

especially when the mouse got control of it all.

[Bridge - Orchestral Breakdown]

[beat cuts]
[low bassoon]
[staccato strings]
[timpani hits]
[water rushing]
[brooms marching]

Voice 1 - Raspy:
They said he was just an apprentice.

Voice 2 - Laughing:
Apprentice?

Voice 1:
Yeah.

Voice 2:
Then why the broom saluting him?

[evil cartoon laugh]

Both:
Brooms...
in the boiler room.

[Verse 3 - Trading Bars]

Voice 1:
Hat too big.

Voice 2:
Spell too strong.

Voice 1:
Broom too wild.

Voice 2:
Night too long.

Voice 1:
Water too high.

Voice 2:
Door won’t move.

Voice 1:
Whole tower drowning.

Voice 2:
Still in groove.

Voice 1:
Strings go sharp.

Voice 2:
Bassoon go doom.

Voice 1:
Timpani hit.

Voice 2:
Oboes fill the room.

Voice 1:
One little mouse.

Voice 2:
Big mistake.

Voice 1:
One little wand.

Voice 2:
Whole world shake.

Both:
Black-and-white world but the money still gold,
whole crew slipping while the spell takes hold.

No crown, no throne,
just a hat and a grin,

then the broomsticks rise and the nightmare begins.

[Final Chorus - Bigger Raspy Gang Chant]

Brooms in the boiler room,
water starts to rise,
little mouse grinning with the stars in his eyes.

Brooms in the boiler room,
knocking at the door,
spell got loose and it hungry for more.

Black-and-white world,
but the money still gold,
whole tower shaking when the wild drums roll.

Brooms in the boiler room,
moon turned blue,
one wrong spell and the whole crew doomed.

Brooms in the boiler room,
still marching,
still stomping,
still pouring,
still watching.

[Outro - Old Film Ending]

[orchestra fades]
[water drains slowly]
[broomstick scrape]
[one final magic chime]

Voice 2:
You think it’s over?

Voice 1:
Nah.

Voice 2:
Why?

Voice 1:
The hat still glowing.

[film reel snaps]

pages.vocalStyle: Aggressive raspy male rap duo with crazy street-cartoon vocals

members.name: LEAD VOX
members.bio: Sings raspy verses while ignoring every warning given by fate itself.

members.name: BACKING VOX
members.bio: Whispers crazy secrets about the spell before it consumed them all.

members.name: DRUMS
members.bio: Keeps time with frantic beats until panic sets in completely now.

members.name: PERCUSSION
members.bio: Marches on broomsticks like a terrifying army of cleaning tools today.

members.name: BASS
members.bio: Grooves low and heavy to shake the tower floorboards right down.

members.name: SYNTH
members.bio: Weaves dark textures that bend moonlight into shadows all around here.

members.name: TEXTURES
members.bio: Adds gritty grit to make every shadow look hungry for more now.

members.name: WOODWINDS
members.bio: Howls with bassoon riffs while oboes scream in cartoon horror tonight.

members.name: BRASS
members.bio: Brings dramatic strings and brass hits to raise the panic levels high.

levels.name: UNEASE REALIZATION
levels.emotion: Inquisitive unease, sudden realization of wrongness

levels.name: FULL DISASTER NOW
levels.emotion: Full-blown disaster with frantic, cartoonish horror

levels.name: SLIPPERY INEVITABILITY
levels.emotion: Narrative tension with a sense of slippery inevitability

levels.name: TOXIC SHIFT PANIC
levels.emotion: Building panic and the toxic shift in atmosphere

levels.name: RESIGNED ACCEPTANCE
levels.emotion: Resigned acceptance of doom amidst rising chaos

levels.name: HYPNOTIC DREAD FATE
levels.emotion: Hypnotic dread and the final realization that help is impossible
